zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Moore於1563年5月3日出生在英國英格蘭肯特坎特布瑞時，他的父親Sir George Moore I27歲，母親Joane Coxsune22歲。他於1612年8月16日與Elizabeth Clarke在紐西蘭坎特布瑞坎特布瑞結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1632年1月在英國英格蘭肯特享年68歲，埋葬在英國肯特福利頓登。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Moore was born on 3 May 1563, in Canterbury, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir George Moore I, was 27 and his mother, Joane Coxsune, was 22. He married Elizabeth Clarke on 16 August 1612, in Canterbury, Canterbury, New Zealand. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in January 1632, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68, and was buried in Frittenden, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir George Moore est né le 3 mai 1563, à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir George Moore I, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joane Coxsune, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Clarke le 16 août 1612, à Canterbury, Canterbury, Nouvelle-Zélande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en janvier 1632, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre.

August 1612 in Canterbury, Canterbury, Neuseeland Elizabeth Clarke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb im Januar 1632 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Frittenden, Kent, England begraben.

ko.Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent,: 영국 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 알프지
ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Moore(이)가 1563년 5월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir George Moore I(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Coxsune(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1612년 8월 16일 뉴질랜드 캉르버이 캉르버이에서 Elizabeth Clarke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1632년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 프리텐든에 묻혔다.

ru.Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent,: кантирбури ст алфедж, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir George Moore родился 3 мая 1563 г. в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir George Moore I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Coxsune, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Clarke 16 августа 1612 г. в местности кантирбури, кантирбури, Новая Зеландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер январь 1632 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия.
